## Terms of Use\n\nBy using this guest n:
## Terms of Use

By using this guest network, you agree to use the internet connection lawfully and responsibly.

- Do not attempt to access systems or data without authorization.
- Do not disrupt network service for other users.
- The network owner may limit, monitor, or terminate access when necessary.
## Safe Internet Policy\n\nThis guest ne:
## Safe Internet Policy

This guest network is provided with safety controls intended to keep internet access lawful and appropriate.

- Do not use the connection for illegal, harmful, abusive, or disruptive activity.
- Some websites, content categories, or services may be restricted by network policy.
- Contact the network administrator if you believe access has been blocked incorrectly.
## Privacy Notice\n\nPersonal data share:
## Privacy Notice

Personal data shared during verification is processed for guest network access, security, logging, and legal compliance purposes.

- Verification details may include contact information, device address, IP address, access time, and session records.
- Records are retained only for operational, security, and legal requirements.
- Contact the network administrator for privacy requests related to this guest network.

When enabled, devices without a valid se: When enabled, devices without a valid session cookie must verify again even if their IP has an active session. To access session details later at xx:port/session, users should complete verification in a regular browser and then open the session page from that same browser. Otherwise, access can still be moved with the verification cookie, but session details will not be available from a browser that does not have that cookie.

Syslog traffic retention must stay withi: Syslog traffic retention must stay within the applicable legal retention window. Records older than the entered day count are automatically deleted from the database and the configured syslog export directory on disk.
Daily KamuSM timestamp files are written: Daily KamuSM timestamp files are written here as YYYY-MM-DD.log and YYYY-MM-DD.log.tsr.
Automatic export files are written here: Automatic export files are written here as .log files; KamuSM timestamp tokens use the matching .tsr extension.
Automatic export files are written here as .log files; timestamp tokens use the matching .tsr extension.
When enabled, each exported .log file is: When enabled, each exported .log file is also written as a .zip archive. Timestamp request and reply files are included when present.
Delete source files after ZIP is created: Delete source files after ZIP is created
Only applies when ZIP creation is enable: Only applies when ZIP creation is enabled. The .log, .tsq and .tsr source files are removed after the ZIP is created.
Syslog exports are created automatically: Syslog exports are created automatically when syslog logging is enabled. If KamuSM timestamping is enabled, the same export schedule is timestamped.

Syslog exports are created automatically when syslog logging is enabled. Daily exports run at 23:59:59 in the export time zone. If KamuSM timestamping is enabled, the same export schedule is timestamped.
Syslog exports are created automatically when syslog logging is enabled. Daily exports run at 23:59:59 in the export time zone. If timestamping is enabled, the same export schedule is timestamped.
Choose KamuSM for the existing Turkey fl: Choose KamuSM for the existing Turkey flow, generic RFC3161 TSA for EU/US-style providers, or API key mode for RFC3161 services protected by an HTTP key.

Choose KamuSM for the existing Turkey flow, generic RFC3161 TSA for EU/US-style providers, or API key mode for RFC3161 services protected by an HTTP key. When disabling timestamping, you can stamp currently unexported records first; exports created while timestamping is disabled stay unsigned.

Creates one RFC3161 timestamped daily lo: Creates one RFC3161 timestamped daily log after each completed local day. Missed completed days are processed after restart.
Creates an RFC3161 timestamp token for e: Creates an RFC3161 timestamp token for each syslog export. Automatic exports use the selected export interval.
Posts an RFC3161 timestamp query and sto: Posts an RFC3161 timestamp query and stores the returned timestamp reply as .tsr.
Optional HTTP headers for providers that: Optional HTTP headers for providers that require account-specific headers.

Creates sealed CSV, JSONL and manifest p: Creates sealed CSV, JSONL and manifest packages for completed time windows.
On restart, missed completed windows are: On restart, missed completed windows are sealed. Records not received while the service is down cannot be reconstructed.

This will compact the hotspot database w: This will compact the hotspot database with SQLite VACUUM. It does not normally delete records, but interrupted maintenance, disk-full errors or storage problems can risk data loss. The system creates a database backup first; still, continue only after syslog exports are complete and during a quiet maintenance window.

This will compact the hotspot database with SQLite VACUUM. It does not delete records, but it can take time on large traffic databases. The system creates a database backup first; run it during a quiet maintenance window.
